title: SkyNRG Secures Key Environmental Approvals to Advance Sustainable Aviation Fuel in Eastern Washington
content: SkyNRG announced a key milestone for Project Wigeon, a planned sustainable aviation fuel facility in Eastern Washington, after securing environmental approvals from the Washington State Department of Ecology and Walla Walla County. These approvals confirm that the project meets state and local environmental standards, including requirements related to land use, water protection, and fuel transportation. Project Wigeon will be among North Americaâ€™s first commercialâ€‘scale SAF projects producing jet fuel from renewable natural gas. The agreement moves the project into the engineering phase, on track for operations to start by 2030. Key aspects include a closedâ€‘loop industrial water system with Zero Liquid Discharge, water conservation using the Port of Walla Walla system, onâ€‘site energy production utilizing byâ€‘products and excess steam, stringent air permit conditions with ongoing monitoring, engineered stormwater management, and transportation safety and coordination for rail and trucks. Fuel produced at the Walla Walla site will use RNG feedstocks, otherwise known as biogenic methane, collected from domestic landfills, wastewater treatment facilities, and manure from animal farms, delivered through existing infrastructure. SAF produced at Project Wigeon can reduce lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions up to 85 percent compared to fossil jet fuel. Washington stateâ€™s leadership is reinforced by Senate Bill 5447 and longstanding work through the Washington State Alternative Jet Fuels Workgroup, with a 1.5 million dollar grant from the Department of Commerce to the Port of Walla Walla supporting construction.
author: Analysts Account
